commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And there was not a man left in Ai or Bethel, that went not out after Israel: and they left the city open, and pursued after Israel. Not one fit to bear arms. (Worthington) Bethel. As soon as the people of this city perceived the Israelites fleeing, they rushed out to assist the king of Hai in the pursuit. But when they saw the former rally, before they had joined their friends, (Calmet) they very prudently retired, and left the unhappy citizens of Hai to their fate. (Haydock) Hence all who were slain belonged to the latter city, ver.
